key_insight: A compact Qwen2.5-7B student can gain meaningful math-reasoning performance through Chain-of-Thought distillation from DeepSeek-R1, but only when training is carefully limited to avoid overfitting.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that LoRA-based distillation on Apple Silicon with MLX can be a practical pathway for deploying smaller reasoning models without fully sacrificing accuracy.
method: Built a CoT training corpus from John O'Bryan Mathematics Competition problems (2011–2025) using a dual-agent pipeline.
Fine-tuned Qwen2.5-7B with LoRA on Apple Silicon via MLX.
Evaluated across competition data, MATH-500, and reasoning-length levels R1–R6.
evidence_signal: Across five 200-iteration runs, the fine-tuned student reached 69.43% mean accuracy on the competition set and 73.1% on MATH-500, versus 64.67% for the base model and 91.40% for the teacher.
watch_item: A 1,000-iteration run overfit quickly, with validation loss bottoming out around iteration 200, so training length and response length need careful control.
paper_title: Knowledge Distillation from Large Reasoning Models to Compact Student Models: A Case Study on the John O Bryan Mathematics Competition
